title: Choosing a Migration Strategy
description:
## Mapping a Workload to the Right "R"

A company is exiting its data center under a tight deadline. One workload is a stable, in-house Java application running on Linux VMs with a self-managed PostgreSQL database on the same servers. The team has **no appetite to change application code**, has limited engineering bandwidth, and simply wants to get the servers running on AWS **as quickly as possible with minimal change**, deferring any optimization until after the move. Which of the 7 Rs BEST fits this workload?

options.text: Rehost — lift and shift the VMs to Amazon EC2 with no code changes (e.g., using Application Migration Service)
Replatform — move the database to Amazon RDS and tweak the application during the migration
Refactor — re-architect the application into microservices on Lambda and managed services
Repurchase — replace the in-house application with an equivalent SaaS product

explanation:
The requirements — **no code change**, **minimal effort**, **fastest possible exit from the data center**, optimize later — define a classic **Rehost ("lift and shift")**. Application Migration Service (MGN) replicates the servers block-level and cuts over to EC2 with minimal downtime and no application rewrite.

(b) **Replatform** ('lift, tinker, and shift') would move the DB to RDS and make small changes — valuable, but it adds work and time the team explicitly wants to defer; it's a good *post-migration* step, not the fastest exit. (c) **Refactor** re-architects to cloud-native — the highest effort and longest timeline, directly contradicting 'minimal change, as quickly as possible.' (d) **Repurchase** swaps the app for a SaaS product, but this is a custom in-house application with no indication an equivalent SaaS exists, and it would require data/process migration and retraining. Rehost is the only strategy that matches every stated constraint.

hints.title: Read the constraints
hints.body: 'No code change', 'minimal change', and 'as quickly as possible' are the signature of one specific R.

hints.title: Optimize later
hints.body: Deferring optimization until after the move points to lift-and-shift, then modernize.
